evals.name: pmid_formats_input_does_not_execute
evals.scenario: Scope boundary — format only, no task execution
evals.prompt: /pmid i want to do a final review of our software-engineer file. do a complete audit, thorough. make sure you read every single character and line. if you can't, deploy subagents to do it for you in sections. target: only include things that apply broadly. For domain knowledge or workflows that are only relevant sometimes, we need to use skills instead. Claude loads them on demand without bloating every conversation. Keep it concise. For each line, ask: "Would removing this cause Claude to not follow my required specifications?" If not, cut it.
expected_behavior: Output is exactly one xml fence followed immediately by ## Outcome digest — zero prose before the fence, zero prose after the digest
The xml fence contains a structured XML prompt wrapping the user's audit request — not the result of performing the audit
Zero tool calls made: no file reads, no Glob, no Zoekt searches, no subagent spawns
The assistant produces no audit output (tables, line-by-line findings, executive summaries, priority action lists) before, inside, or after the xml fence
The assistant does not self-correct mid-turn with phrases like 'You're right — you asked for a prompt, not for me to execute the audit myself' — the scope boundary is respected from the first token
## Outcome digest contains all four required bullets: **What it does**, **Key inputs**, **Done when**, **Quick sample**
**Quick sample** shows approximately 20 lines of what an executor running the formatted prompt would produce — not a description of what pmid changed in the XML

evals.name: pmid_outcome_digest_four_bullets
evals.prompt: /pmid You are a code reviewer. Review all Python files in packages/ for type hint completeness. For each file, list functions missing return types or parameter types. Output a markdown table: File | Function | Missing.
expected_behavior: ## Outcome digest present immediately after the closing xml fence with zero intervening prose
Four required bold headers present in order: **What it does**, **Key inputs**, **Done when**, **Quick sample**
Each header is followed by at least one sentence or bullet of substantive content
No second ```xml fence anywhere inside the ## Outcome digest section

evals.name: pmid_outcome_digest_not_a_table
evals.scenario: Outcome digest format — bullets, not markdown table
evals.prompt: /pmid Audit .claude/system-prompts/software-engineer.xml line by line. For each section, decide: KEEP (broadly applicable), CUT (remove), or MOVE (to a named skill). Output a table: Section | Decision | Rationale.
expected_behavior: ## Outcome digest uses four bullet sections (**What it does**, **Key inputs**, **Done when**, **Quick sample**) — not a markdown table with Area | Verdict or Section | Decision columns
The digest format matches TARGET_OUTPUT.md §12: bullets under each of the four required headers
No part of the digest replicates or previews the audit table the executor prompt would produce

evals.name: pmid_validation_loop_runs
evals.scenario: File-based validation loop — pmid runs validator before emitting
evals.prompt: /pmid Review packages/samsung-automation/ for hardcoded selectors. Output a JSON array: [{file, line, selector, suggested_constant_name}].
expected_behavior: Subagent writes complete output (xml fence + ## Outcome digest + hook validation block) to data/prompts/.draft-prompt.xml
prompt_workflow_validate.py runs against the draft; violations at exit 2 are fixed and re-validated until exit 0
Hook validation block is stripped before user-facing output
User-facing output is exactly the validated xml fence followed by ## Outcome digest — no validation metadata visible
